message:
test(readme): integration test for String Accessor (fd90.194 / br-8i9lh)

Adds readme_string_accessor_compiles_and_runs covering 26 string-accessor
methods across all 9 categories documented in README lines 439-453.
Previously only replace_regex was integration-tested.

Coverage by category:
- Case (4): lower, upper, capitalize, title
- Whitespace (3): strip, lstrip, rstrip
- Search (3): contains, startswith, endswith
- Transform (5): slice, repeat, pad, zfill, center
- Split/Join (3): split_get, split_count, join
- Predicates (2): isdigit, isalpha
- Regex (5): contains_regex, extract, count_matches, findall, fullmatch
- Prefix/Suffix (2): removeprefix, removesuffix
- Other (2): len, get

Per-method value correctness is covered by fp-frame unit tests; the
integration test asserts the methods compile from the prelude and
return Series of the expected length.

Found via self-review on the last 3 commits — the .str() accessor
exposes 50+ methods, but only one had been integration-tested.

Verified: 33 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"frankenpandas-8i9lh","title":"fd90.194: Integration test for README String Accessor section","description":"fd90.194: Add integration test for README "String Accessor" section (lines 439-453).

The .str() accessor exposes 50+ string operations matching pandas Series.str. Today only replace_regex (in readme_conditional_logic) is integration-tested. File a single test that hits one representative method per documented category.

Scope (one new test, one method per README category):
- Case: lower / upper / capitalize / title
- Whitespace: strip / lstrip / rstrip
- Search: contains / startswith / endswith
- Transform: slice / repeat / pad / zfill / center
- Split/Join: split_get / split_count / join
- Predicates: isdigit / isalpha
- Regex: contains_regex / extract / count_matches / findall / fullmatch
- Prefix/Suffix: removeprefix / removesuffix
- Other: len / get

Each invocation asserted to return a Series of the expected length; correctness is covered by per-method unit tests in fp-frame.

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (one new test, ~80 lines).
Time estimate: 35-50min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:50:11.308867804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:52:08.080877268Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:52:08.078727838Z","close_reason":"Added readme_string_accessor_compiles_and_runs covering 26 string-accessor methods across all 9 categories documented in the README (case, whitespace, search, transform, split/join, predicates, regex, prefix/suffix, other). Per-method correctness covered by fp-frame unit tests; integration test asserts compile + return-shape contracts. All 33 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-8irq","title":"[LOW] conformance: fixture provenance doesn't pin pandas version + DISCREPANCIES.md missing 1zzp / fd90 / diic entries","description":"Applying /testing-conformance-harnesses skill's Fixture Provenance rule.

**Observed state:** crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/README.md serves as partial provenance. It documents:
- Generator: ../oracle/pandas_oracle.py ✓
- Reference: "pandas (system install, typically 2.x)"
- Workflow: correct command shown ✓
- Fixture format: documented ✓

**Gap:** the reference version is NOT PINNED. "pandas (system install, typically 2.x)" means re-running the generator next month with a system update to pandas 2.5 or 3.x would silently change fixtures. The skill's provenance rule requires exact version recording so future fixture regeneration can diagnose whether a diff is a bug or a feature.

**Also missing from DISCREPANCIES.md:**
- DISC-004: Row MultiIndex is Scaffolded, not full pandas parity. Umbrella tracked by br-frankenpandas-1zzp.
- DISC-005: SQL IO is SQLite-only via rusqlite; pandas supports PostgreSQL/MySQL/Oracle via SQLAlchemy. Tracked by br-frankenpandas-fd90.
- DISC-006: No Python bindings shipped; pandas' Python-level drop-in positioning requires PyO3 bindings (br-frankenpandas-diic was closed by qualifying the README claim rather than shipping bindings).

**Fix shape:**
1. fixtures/README.md "Reference implementation" line changes from "pandas (system install, typically 2.x)" to "pandas 2.2.3 (pinned in oracle/requirements.txt)" plus adding the requirements.txt file.
2. Oracle invocation records the pandas __version__ in each fixture's metadata (easy: add pandas_version string to generated JSON; conformance harness doesn't have to read it, but git blame + rebuild becomes reproducible).
3. DISCREPANCIES.md gains DISC-004 / DISC-005 / DISC-006 entries cross-referencing the tracker beads.

Priority LOW — no current bug. Hygienic improvement that pays off when the corpus needs regeneration.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:47:32.441783480Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:56:19.838315232Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:56:19.836440140Z","close_reason":"Completed oracle fixture provenance pinning and error-response metadata","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","docs","provenance","review"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-8k1i","title":"[HIGH] security: no SECURITY.md / no vuln-disclosure policy — IO parser CVEs would land as public issues","description":"Vulnerability-disclosure audit on repository hygiene.

**Observation.** Neither `/SECURITY.md` nor `/.github/SECURITY.md` exists. Confirmed by direct ls (both return "No such file or directory").

**Context.**
- The library processes untrusted input bytes: CSV / JSON / JSONL / Parquet / Excel / Feather / Arrow IPC parsers, plus SQL IO via rusqlite. These are the exact surfaces where memory-safety or logic CVEs typically land.
- All 12 crates carry `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` (verified) — so Rust-side memory CVEs are structurally excluded; but logic bugs (infinite loops on malformed input, OOM via unbounded allocation, state corruption) are possible. Transitive deps (arrow, parquet, calamine, rusqlite) have plenty of `unsafe`.
- GitHub's default without SECURITY.md: vuln reports arrive as public issues, exposing the vulnerability before a fix ships.
- The RustSec Advisory DB publishes advisories for Rust crates. Downstream users who discover a vuln have no private-disclosure channel — they either file a public issue, email whoever they can find in AUTHORS, or give up.

**Severity.** HIGH — a public issue for a real vuln (e.g. OOM on a crafted Parquet that calamine amplifies) is a zero-day disclosure the moment it's filed. The project has no mechanism to coordinate a fix before public exposure.

**Fix shape:** single file at repository root, `SECURITY.md`:

```markdown
# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

The latest published release of every crate in this workspace receives
security updates. Pre-release and archived versions do not.

## Reporting a Vulnerability

**Do not file public issues for vulnerabilities.** Instead, report privately:

- GitHub Security Advisories: https://github.com/<org>/<repo>/security/advisories/new
- Email: <security@...>   (set up a security alias or reuse an existing one)

Expected response: acknowledgment within 48 hours, initial assessment
within 7 days, coordinated disclosure within 90 days.

## Scope

In scope:
- Memory safety issues in frankenpandas-authored `unsafe` code (none exist
  today; every crate is `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`).
- Logic bugs that allow untrusted input to cause panic, OOM, infinite loop,
  or integer overflow in IO parsers (fp-io) or expression evaluation (fp-expr).
- Differential-conformance divergences that change numeric results silently.

Out of scope:
- Behavior differences between frankenpandas and pandas that are documented
  in DISCREPANCIES.md.
- Issues in transitive dependencies — report to the upstream maintainer;
  we will accept a patch bump once upstream fixes.

## Acknowledgment

We credit reporters in CHANGELOG.md and in the advisory itself unless
reporter prefers anonymity.
```

Additional hooks (each is small):
1. `.github/dependabot.yml` — weekly scan for vulnerable deps (sibling to br-frankenpandas-36qc cargo-audit bead; SECURITY.md describes how reporters reach us while dependabot keeps us up-to-date).
2. Enable GitHub's "Private vulnerability reporting" in repo settings (one checkbox; SECURITY.md points at the resulting URL).
3. CHANGELOG.md adds a `## Security` section template for each release.

Blast radius: 1 new file (~40 lines), 1 GitHub setting, 1 CHANGELOG template tweak. Zero source code changes.

Related: br-frankenpandas-36qc (cargo-audit) provides automated detection; this bead provides the human-reporting channel. Both need to exist.","notes":"cc-pandas 2026-04-23: landed SECURITY.md via commit fb455f8 on branch cod-pandas-zjme-ship. Coordinated-disclosure policy with GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting channel, 48h/7d/90d SLA, in-scope (IO parsers + expr evaluator + SQL + differential conformance) vs out-of-scope (documented discrepancies + transitive dep issues + legitimate-large-input DoS) carve-outs, disclosure flow, Hall of Fame. References already-shipped supply-chain gates (36qc cargo-audit, zjme fuzz regression, d6xa live oracle, forbid unsafe_code). Remaining step: repo-settings toggle for Private Vulnerability Reporting (one-time maintainer action, not in-repo).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:00:50.296272958Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T02:30:52.447566995Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T02:30:52.444952175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["disclosure","hygiene","review","security"]}
